main.description: A comment on a fyi.requested.document. Lives on the commenter's PDS, not the document author's. May be anchored to a specific line of a specific version (line comment) or to the document as a whole (document-level comment).

document.description: AT-URI of the fyi.requested.document being commented on. Mutable target (the doc's currentVersion changes over time), so this is a plain at-uri rather than a strongRef.

version.description: Strong ref to the specific fyi.requested.documentVersion the commenter actually saw when leaving the comment. Versions are immutable, so this CID is stable; the view UI can compare it against the document's current version to detect when a line comment was made against an older snapshot.

line.description: 1-indexed line number in the pinned version's body. Omit for a document-level comment that is not anchored to a specific line.

body.description: Markdown source of the comment text. Rendered client-side; no embedded HTML is trusted.

parent.description: Strong ref to a parent fyi.requested.comment, forming a reply thread. Absent for top-level comments. Reserved for the threading feature — current UI does not yet render replies, but the field is in the schema from day one to avoid migration.

suggestion.description: Optional inline edit suggestion. When present, the comment proposes replacing `target` (uniquely anchored by `before` + `target` + `after` in the pinned version's body) with `replacement`. The document author may apply the suggestion as a new version; rendering otherwise is informational. Anchoring is text-based rather than line-based so that applying earlier suggestions doesn't invalidate later ones — each apply just re-anchors against the new body.

A textual replacement proposal anchored by surrounding context. To apply, locate `before` + `target` + `after` in the current body — if the match is unique, replace the `target` span with `replacement`. The before/after context is captured at write time from the pinned version's body and is bounded so the record stays small.

before.description: Body text immediately preceding `target`. Provides anchoring context; may be empty when the target is at the start of the document.

target.description: The exact body text the suggestion proposes to replace. Captured verbatim from the pinned version.

after.description: Body text immediately following `target`. Provides anchoring context; may be empty when the target is at the end of the document.

replacement.description: What the commenter proposes to replace `target` with. May be empty (a deletion suggestion).
